title: Supervisor module structure: budget + socket + main
rationale: Phase 12's supervisor is split into three files so each piece is unit-testable in isolation: budget.ts (RestartBudget sliding-window + backoffMs), socket.ts (waitForSocket polling), and main.ts (orchestrator wiring). The orchestrator takes a SupervisorDeps interface (spawn, waitForSocket, installSignalHandler, sleep, now, logger) so tests drive it through fakes without spawning real processes. Production wiring lives in productionDeps().

content: Supervisor restart-policy defaults match SPEC §10.3: 5 restarts in 60s, exponential backoff (1s base, 16s cap, doubling per attempt — totals 31s across 5 attempts, fitting inside the 60s window). 5s SIGTERM grace before SIGKILL. After budget exhaustion the supervisor exits non-zero so docker/fly's restart policy bounces the whole container.

Supervisor env vars (warren-namespaced): WARREN_BURROW_SOCKET (where supervisor binds burrow's socket — also read by warren-the-server, default /var/run/burrow.sock), WARREN_BURROW_BIN (burrow binary, default 'burrow'), WARREN_SUPERVISOR_BUN (bun binary path, default 'bun'), WARREN_SERVER_ENTRY (warren server entry, default 'src/server/main.ts'). Defaults match the canonical Dockerfile layout; overrides exist so the supervisor can boot on a host outside the container.

title: warren exit propagates; burrow exit triggers restart
rationale: Asymmetry baked into runSupervisor: warren is the user-facing process and any warren exit (zero or non-zero) tears down the supervisor with warren's exit code, since SPEC §10.3 prefers restart-by-orchestrator over in-process retry to avoid masking warren bugs. Burrow is the runtime substrate and a non-zero exit triggers a restart (within budget). A clean (zero) exit from burrow without a shutdown signal is treated as fatal — burrow shouldn't exit zero spontaneously, so restarting it would mask a real bug.

name: SupervisorDeps injection seam
description: runSupervisor takes a SupervisorDeps bundle ({spawn, waitForSocket, installSignalHandler, sleep, now, logger}) so tests drive the orchestrator through fakes (queued FakeChild objects, manual signal triggers, instant sleep, pinned now) without spawning real processes. Production wiring lives in productionDeps() and uses Bun.spawn / fs.access / process.on / Date.now / pino. Same pattern as ServerDeps in src/server/types.ts.

name: boot-time git insteadOf install
description: Supervisor's import.meta.main block calls installGitCredentials() before runSupervisor() so 'git config --global url.https://x-access-token:$GITHUB_TOKEN@github.com/.insteadOf https://github.com/' is in /root/.gitconfig before burrow + warren spawn. Without it the reap step's 'git push' fails (no credential helper inside the container) and project clones over HTTPS prompt for a password. No-op when GITHUB_TOKEN is unset; throws and exits 1 if git config returns non-zero (we don't want a silent boot with a broken credential rewrite). The token is only logged as 'using GITHUB_TOKEN' (no value).

content: Supervisor splices --no-auth and extra burrow args from env: WARREN_BURROW_NO_AUTH=1 (or "true", case-insensitive) appends --no-auth to `burrow serve`, and WARREN_BURROW_ARGS is whitespace-split and appended after. Both live in resolveCommandFromEnv (src/supervisor/main.ts) — same boolean parsing convention as WARREN_DISABLE_UI ("1"/"true"). Order is fixed: [bin, serve, --socket, path, --no-auth?, ...args] so callers get a deterministic command vector. Use NO_AUTH for the loopback-only dev case where BURROW_API_TOKEN is unset; ARGS is the escape hatch for flags warren does not yet model explicitly.

description: src/supervisor/git-credentials.ts writes the insteadOf rewrite rule to /root/.gitconfig because supervisor HOME=/root. Burrow's bwrap profile ro-binds /usr, /etc, /lib, /lib64, /bin, /sbin, /opt only — NOT /root. Sandboxed agents at UID 1000 can't see /root/.gitconfig, so any git operation against github.com from inside the sandbox fails with 'could not read Username'. Same architectural pattern as warren-1eaa (bun store at /root/.bun invisible). The gitconfig install was scoped to host-side reap (warren-dcf3 closed) and never extended into sandbox.
resolution: Two acceptable shapes: (A) relocate the gitconfig write target to /etc/gitconfig or another path inside burrow's bwrap ro-bind set — supervisor still owns the rewrite, but it's now visible inside sandboxes. (B) keep gitconfig host-only and codify the V1 contract: agent commits, reap pushes; canopy prompts must NOT instruct sandboxed agents to push. For V1, B is recommended (warren-1a09 fix scopes A+D). Tracked as warren-1a09 (P2).

description: Deploying warren to Fly without setting BOTH BURROW_API_TOKEN (server-side, read by 'burrow serve') AND WARREN_BURROW_TOKEN (client-side, read by src/burrow-client/config.ts:42) to the SAME value causes one of two failure modes: (1) BURROW_API_TOKEN unset → burrow child crashes with [validation_error], supervisor times out waiting for the unix socket, machine boot-loops 10x and Fly stops restarting it; (2) BURROW_API_TOKEN set but WARREN_BURROW_TOKEN unset/different → burrow comes up healthy, dispatch 401s with 'missing Authorization header' from withTransportMapping in src/burrow-client/client.ts:112, surfaces as a generic 500 in the UI.
resolution: Set both secrets to the same value in one shot: TOKEN=$(openssl rand -hex 32); fly secrets set BURROW_API_TOKEN=$TOKEN WARREN_BURROW_TOKEN=$TOKEN -a warren-deployed. Verify with 'fly ssh console -C "sh -c \"test \\\"\$BURROW_API_TOKEN\\\" = \\\"\$WARREN_BURROW_TOKEN\\\" && echo match\""'. fly.toml comment block updated to call this out; warren-d317 tracks supervisor-side fail-fast validation.
